title: 1986 oil price collapse
description: Saudi Arabia abandons its swing-producer role; oil prices crash from ~$27 to under $10/barrel, straining every oil-exporting economy in this database (Saudi Arabia, Venezuela, USSR, Iran).

title: Fall of the Berlin Wall
description: East Germany opens its borders, triggering the collapse of Communist governments across Eastern Europe and setting up the transition from centrally planned to market economies across the Eastern Bloc — the same transition Russia undergoes on a larger scale after 1991.

title: Dissolution of the Soviet Union
description: USSR dissolves into 15 successor states; Russia inherits most Soviet economic institutions and begins a chaotic market-transition (shock therapy") in 1992."

title: Mexican "Tequila Crisis"
description: Mexico devalues and then floats the peso, which loses roughly half its value within months; the resulting emerging-market contagion triggers a bank run and 1995 recession in Argentina (see Argentina entry) and capital flight across Latin America and Asia.

title: World Trade Organization founded
description: The WTO succeeds the 1948 GATT under the Marrakesh Agreement, creating a binding dispute-settlement system and expanding multilateral trade rules to services and intellectual property; membership becomes a major economic-policy goal (and geopolitical lever) for most of this database's countries over

title: Asian Financial Crisis begins
description: Thai baht collapse triggers contagion across East Asia (see South Korea entry for the IMF program specifics).

title: Russian default and LTCM crisis trigger global contagion
description: Russia defaults on domestic debt, imposes a 90-day moratorium on foreign creditors, and devalues the ruble; the shock triggers the near-collapse of US hedge fund Long-Term Capital Management (requiring a $3.6bn bailout) and spreads volatility through bond and equity markets in both emerging and deve

title: Euro launched as accounting currency
description: Eleven EU members (including Spain and Portugal; Greece follows in 2001) lock exchange rates and adopt the euro for electronic and financial transactions, with notes and coins entering circulation in 2002 — the largest peacetime monetary-union experiment in history.

title: China WTO accession
description: China's WTO entry accelerates global manufacturing relocation, indirectly affecting trade balances and appliance-manufacturing competitiveness for European producers in this database (Spain, Portugal, Greece).

title: 2000s commodity super-cycle
description: China's post-2001 WTO-driven infrastructure boom, alongside strong global growth, drives the IMF commodity price index up roughly fourfold between January 2000 and mid-2008; crude oil rises from about $30/barrel in 2003 to a record $147/barrel on 11 July 2008, delivering a sustained fiscal windfall

title: Iraq War begins
description: US-led invasion halts roughly 2 million barrels/day of Iraqi oil production; global crude prices spike toward $40/barrel before Saudi Arabia and other OPEC members raise output to offset the loss, averaging $30/barrel for 2003 overall (up 19% from 2002).

title: Global Financial Crisis — Lehman Brothers collapse
description: Triggers a synchronized global recession; oil prices crash from ~$147 to ~$40/barrel within months, hitting every oil exporter in this database simultaneously, while credit-driven European economies (Spain, Portugal, Greece) enter prolonged crises.

title: European sovereign debt crisis
description: Greece (and to a lesser extent Portugal and Spain) enters an IMF/EU bailout program after a fiscal-sustainability crisis, reshaping two of this database's European comparators for a decade.

title: Arab Spring begins
description: Protests beginning in Tunisia in December 2010 spread across the Middle East and North Africa; resulting production disruptions (over 2 million barrels/day lost across Libya, Syria, Yemen, Tunisia and Sudan) push Brent crude from $92 to $120/barrel by April 2011, benefiting Saudi Arabia's fiscal pos

title: 2014-2016 oil price collapse
description: Oil prices fall from ~$115 to below $30/barrel amid US shale supply growth and OPEC's decision not to cut output; a major driver of Venezuela's and Russia's subsequent crises, and a fiscal shock for Saudi Arabia and Iran.

title: Iran nuclear deal (JCPOA) signed
description: Iran and the P5+1 finalize the Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action in Vienna, exchanging nuclear-program limits for the lifting of UN, EU and US nuclear-related sanctions; roughly $100bn in frozen Iranian assets are released after IAEA-verified implementation begins in January 2016.

title: US withdraws from the JCPOA
description: President Trump announces US withdrawal from the Iran nuclear deal and directs the phased reimposition of all sanctions lifted in 2015-16, with full "snapback" effective 5 November 2018, reversing the 2015 sanctions-relief framework and re-isolating Iran's oil and banking sectors from the dollar sys

title: COVID-19 declared a pandemic
description: WHO declaration triggers synchronized global lockdowns, an oil-demand collapse (WTI briefly trades negative on 20 April 2020), and unprecedented fiscal/monetary stimulus across every country in this database.

title: WTI oil futures trade negative
description: COVID-19 demand collapse combined with the March 2020 Saudi-Russia price war and near-full storage capacity at the Cushing, Oklahoma hub drive the US WTI May futures contract to an unprecedented settlement of -$37.63/barrel, the starkest single data point of the pandemic-era oil-demand shock.

title: Russian invasion of Ukraine
description: Triggers sweeping Western sanctions on Russia, a global energy-price shock benefiting other oil/gas exporters (Iran, Saudi Arabia, Venezuela partially re-engaged by the West for supply), and a European inflation surge affecting Spain, Portugal, Greece.

title: Fastest Federal Reserve tightening cycle since the 1980s
description: The Fed raises its policy rate by 5.25 percentage points between March 2022 and July 2023 to fight post-pandemic inflation, its most aggressive tightening since the early 1980s; the resulting dollar strength and capital outflows push several emerging-market currencies in this database down more than

title: Metaxas dictatorship (4th of August Regime) begins
description: General Ioannis Metaxas suspends parliament and imposes martial law with King George II's backing, establishing an authoritarian, anti-communist corporatist state that lasts until his death in 1941.

title: Greek Civil War
description: Communist-led insurgents and the Western-backed government fight a full-scale civil war from 1946 to October 1949, on top of WWII occupation devastation; postwar reconstruction is delayed roughly a decade and financed largely through US Marshall Plan and Truman Doctrine aid.

title: Truman Doctrine — US aid begins
description: President Truman requests $400m in military and economic assistance for Greece and Turkey after Britain says it can no longer fund the fight against the communist-led insurgency; Congress approves $300m for Greece, launching sustained US involvement in Greek reconstruction and the broader Cold War a

title: Drachma devaluation and stabilization
description: Minister Spyros Markezinis devalues the drachma 50% against the dollar and curbs import restrictions, achieving Greece's first balanced budget since the war; industrial production rises nearly 40% in 1953 and a 1954 revaluation (1 new drachma = 1,000 old) anchors two decades of currency stability un
